# ThriveStack Product Telemetry — AI Instrumentation Guide
# Version: 3.2
# Hosted at: https://docs.thrivestack.ai/product/setup/llms.txt

---

## PURPOSE

This file gives AI coding assistants complete, executable instructions for instrumenting
any existing web app with ThriveStack product telemetry.
Every rule, schema, and example here is authoritative — follow exactly, do not deviate.

All event schemas are non-exhaustive. Additional properties beyond those listed are always allowed.

---

## HOW TO USE THIS GUIDE

Work through these phases in order. Do not write any code until Phase 0 is complete.

### Phase 0 — Understand the codebase first
1. Identify the tech stack, auth library, and state management
2. Find `userId` — see STEP 2 for search patterns and rules
3. Find `groupId` — see STEP 2 for hierarchy, search patterns, and fallbacks
4. Find all instrumentable feature actions — ask the developer to classify each as `"core"`, `"sticky"`, or `"support"` before writing any code

### Phase 1 — Install the script tag
Add once to the root layout. Follow STEP 1 exactly for the detected framework.

### Phase 2 — Instrument lifecycle events
Follow the FIRING ORDER section exactly for each flow found: signup, login, invite.

### Phase 3 — Instrument feature events
One `feature_used` event per confirmed feature action. `feature_name` in `[noun]_[verb]` snake_case.

### Phase 4 — Verify then summarise
Run through the VERIFICATION CHECKLIST. Fix anything missing. Then output the summary.

**REQUIRED OUTPUT FORMAT — strictly follow this, no exceptions.**
Do NOT write a narrative summary. Do NOT output a "Summary of Changes" section. Do NOT describe what you did in prose.
Your only output after instrumentation is complete is the table below, printed inline in the chat. No files. No markdown files. No prose before or after it.

```
ThriveStack Instrumentation Summary
X/9 implemented

| # | Event | Status | Notes |
|---|-------|--------|-------|
| 1 | identify | ✅/⚠️/❌ | Field used, file |
| 2 | group | ✅/⚠️/❌ | Entity levels, billing root |
| 3 | signed_up | ✅/⚠️/❌ | Handler, file |
| 4 | signed_in | ✅/⚠️/❌ | Handler, file |
| 5 | account_created | ✅/⚠️/❌ | Handler, file or N/A |
| 6 | account_added_user | ✅/⚠️/❌ | Entry points covered |
| 7 | feature_used | ✅/⚠️/❌ | Features and their feature_type |
| 8 | invite_sent | ✅/⚠️/❌ | Handler, file or N/A |
| 9 | onboarding_step_completed | ✅/⚠️/❌ | Steps found or N/A |

User ID: [field] in [file]
Group hierarchy: [e.g. Organization → Account (billing root) → Workspace] or [B2C fallback]
Unresolved: [anything skipped and why, or "None"]

---
Handy ThriveStack Resources
- Setup Progress: https://app.thrivestack.ai/analyze/onboarding/product-analytics/telemetry
- Diagnostics: https://app.thrivestack.ai/analyze/diagnostics-report
- API / Audit Logs: https://app.thrivestack.ai/analyze/events
- Developer Tools: https://app.thrivestack.ai/analyze/developer-tool
```

---

## STEP 1 — INSTALL THE SCRIPT TAG

This is the ONLY initialization method. There is no npm package. There is no init() call.

Add once to the root layout file. Never inside a component or page.

### Next.js App Router (app/layout.tsx)
```tsx
import Script from 'next/script'
export default function RootLayout({ children }) {
  return (
    <html>
      <head>
        <Script
          src="https://ts-script.app.thrivestack.ai/latest/thrivestack.js"
          strategy="afterInteractive"
          data-api-key={process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_THRIVESTACK_API_KEY}
          data-source="product"
        />
      </head>
      <body>{children}</body>
    </html>
  )
}
```

### React (public/index.html)
```html
<script
  src="https://ts-script.app.thrivestack.ai/latest/thrivestack.js"
  api-key="%REACT_APP_THRIVESTACK_API_KEY%"
  source="product">
</script>
```

### Vue / Nuxt / Plain HTML
```html
<script
  src="https://ts-script.app.thrivestack.ai/latest/thrivestack.js"
  api-key="YOUR_ENV_VAR_HERE"
  source="product">
</script>
```

Rules:
- Write the API key to the environment variable file appropriate for this stack (.env, .env.local, application.yml, etc.) — never in source code
- `api-key` must reference that environment variable — never hardcoded
- `source="product"` — always this exact string, never change it
- Global object after load: `thriveStack` — camelCase, lowercase 's'
- Place in root layout ONCE only

---

## STEP 2 — FIND userId AND groupId

### userId
Must be a stable unique identifier that does not change across sessions.
May be a UUID, integer, or any format the auth provider uses.
Must NOT be an email, display name, or session token.

Search patterns:
```javascript
const { user } = useAuth()               → user.id
const session = useSession()             → session.user.id
useContext(AuthContext)                  → context.user.id
const { userId } = auth()               → userId  (Clerk)
store.auth.user.id                       → (Redux/Zustand)
claims.sub, claims.user_id              → (JWT)
```

If no stable identifier found — stop and ask:
"What field uniquely identifies a user in your database — their ID, not their email?"

---

### groupId — Entity Hierarchy

Account is always mandatory — it is the billing/contractual root entity.
All other levels are additional and each gets its own `group()` call.

Default hierarchy:
```
Organization   (brand/company umbrella)
  └── Account  ← MANDATORY — billing root
        └── Tenant
              └── Workspace
                    └── Team
```

This is a default — if the developer confirms a different entity is their billing root,
treat that as Account regardless of what it is called in code.

Search patterns:
```
AUTH CONTEXT:
  user.accountId / user.account.id
  user.orgId / user.organizationId / user.organization.id
  user.tenantId / user.workspaceId / user.companyId
  session.organization.id / session.account.id

JWT CUSTOM CLAIMS:
  claims.org_id / claims.account_id / claims.tenant_id
  claims['https://yourapp.com/org_id']   ← Auth0 pattern

STORE (Redux/Zustand/Pinia):
  store.currentOrg.id / store.activeWorkspace.id
  useOrganization().organization.id

POST-LOGIN API RESPONSE:
  loginResponse.organization.id / meResponse.current_organization.id

URL PARAMS:
  /app/:accountId / /org/:orgId / /workspace/:workspaceId
  Note: if only in URL params, ask developer if always available
```

B2C / no-org fallback:
```javascript
group_id       = user.id         // same as userId
account_name   = user.name       // user's full name
account_domain = user.email      // full email address — not just domain
// omit plan_id and plan_name entirely
```
`group()` fires only once on first registration — never again.

Stop and ask if:
- No org-like field found after exhaustive search
- Multiple top-level entities exist and billing root is unclear
- groupId only in URL params and may not always be available

Never proceed without groupId. Never generate a fake ID.

### Context switching
When user switches active workspace/tenant/org:
- Do NOT re-fire `group()`
- Update `context.group_id` to the new entity's ID for all subsequent `track()` calls

---

## STEP 3 — identify()

Call on signup, login, and whenever new/updated user info is gathered.
`user_id` is always mandatory. Send whatever is known at the time.

```javascript
if (typeof thriveStack === 'undefined') return
thriveStack.identify([{
  "user_id": userId,
  "traits": {
    "user_email": user.email,
    "user_name": user.name
  },
  "timestamp": new Date().toISOString()
}])
```

---

## STEP 4 — group()

Fire when an entity is first created. One call per entity level found.
Never fire on login, context switch, or invite acceptance.

### Account (mandatory)
```javascript
if (typeof thriveStack === 'undefined') return
thriveStack.group([{
  "user_id": userId,
  "group_id": account.id,
  "traits": {
    "group_type": "Account",
    "account_domain": account.domain,
    "account_name": account.name,
    "plan_id": account.plan?.id,      // omit if unavailable
    "plan_name": account.plan?.name   // omit if unavailable
  },
  "context": {
    "group_id": account.id,           // must match group_id exactly
    "group_type": "Account"
  },
  "timestamp": new Date().toISOString()
}])
```

### Additional levels (same signature, change group_type and ID)
```javascript
thriveStack.group([{
  "user_id": userId,
  "group_id": org.id,
  "traits": {
    "group_type": "Organization",     // "Organization"|"Tenant"|"Workspace"|"Team"
    "account_domain": org.domain,
    "account_name": org.name
  },
  "context": { "group_id": org.id, "group_type": "Organization" },
  "timestamp": new Date().toISOString()
}])
```

`"Account"` is reserved for the billing root only.

---

## STEP 5 — TRACK EVENTS

### Base signature
```javascript
if (typeof thriveStack === 'undefined') return
thriveStack.track([{
  "event_name": "event_name_here",
  "user_id": userId,
  "properties": {
    "user_email": user.email,
    "user_name": user.name
  },
  "context": { "group_id": groupId },
  "timestamp": new Date().toISOString()
}])
```

---

## EVENT SCHEMAS

### signed_up
When: New user registration completes.
Context: Include if groupId available. Omit if account doesn't exist yet.

```javascript
thriveStack.track([{
  "event_name": "signed_up",
  "user_id": user.id,
  "properties": {
    "user_email": user.email,
    "user_name": user.name,
    "utm_source": getUTMParam('utm_source'),
    "utm_medium": getUTMParam('utm_medium'),
    "utm_campaign": getUTMParam('utm_campaign'),
    "utm_term": getUTMParam('utm_term')
  },
  "context": { "group_id": user.orgId },   // omit if not yet available
  "timestamp": new Date().toISOString()
}])

function getUTMParam(param) {
  return new URLSearchParams(window.location.search).get(param)
    || sessionStorage.getItem(param)
    || null
}
```

---

### signed_in
When: Existing user logs in successfully.
No UTM params. `context` is mandatory — groupId is always available at login.

```javascript
thriveStack.track([{
  "event_name": "signed_in",
  "user_id": user.id,
  "properties": {
    "user_email": user.email,
    "user_name": user.name,
    "user_role": user.role    // omit if unavailable
  },
  "context": { "group_id": groupId },
  "timestamp": new Date().toISOString()
}])
```

---

### account_created
When: New account/org/workspace is first created.
`group()` must fire BEFORE this. Do not fire if joining an existing account.

```javascript
thriveStack.track([{
  "event_name": "account_created",
  "user_id": user.id,
  "properties": {
    "account_id": org.id,
    "account_name": org.name,
    "account_domain": org.domain,
    "user_email": user.email,
    "user_name": user.name,
    "plan_name": org.plan?.name   // omit if unavailable
  },
  "context": { "group_id": org.id },
  "timestamp": new Date().toISOString()
}])
```

---

### account_added_user
When: Account membership grows. Fire in ALL applicable entry points — missing any one is wrong.

Entry points:
1. New user direct signup + new account → after `account_created` → `join_method: "signup"`, `is_first_user: true`
2. New user invited to existing account → after `signed_up` → `join_method: "invite"`, `is_first_user: false`
3. Existing user invited, not yet in account → after `signed_in` → `join_method: "invite"`, `is_first_user: false`
4. SSO/SCIM/admin provisioning → `join_method: "sso"` / `"scim"` / `"admin"`

Do NOT fire if user is already a member of the account.

```javascript
thriveStack.track([{
  "event_name": "account_added_user",
  "user_id": user.id,
  "properties": {
    "user_email": user.email,
    "user_name": user.name,
    "account_id": org.id,
    "account_name": org.name,
    "account_domain": org.domain,
    "role": user.role,
    "join_method": "signup",      // "signup"|"invite"|"sso"|"scim"|"admin"
    "is_first_user": true
  },
  "context": { "group_id": org.id },
  "timestamp": new Date().toISOString()
}])
```

---

### onboarding_step_completed
When: User completes a discrete setup step.
Search for: `<Stepper>` `onStepComplete` `handleNext` `setCurrentStep` `markStepComplete`

```javascript
thriveStack.track([{
  "event_name": "onboarding_step_completed",
  "user_id": user.id,
  "properties": {
    "user_email": user.email,
    "user_name": user.name,
    "step_name": stepName,      // snake_case: "connected_data_source"
    "step_index": stepIndex,    // 0-based integer
    "total_steps": totalSteps
  },
  "context": { "group_id": org.id },
  "timestamp": new Date().toISOString()
}])
```

---

### invite_sent
When: User sends a team invitation.
Search for: `inviteUser` `sendInvite` `inviteTeamMember` — forms with email + role fields.
All properties beyond `user_email` and `invitee_email` are send-if-available.

```javascript
thriveStack.track([{
  "event_name": "invite_sent",
  "user_id": user.id,
  "properties": {
    "user_email": user.email,
    "invitee_email": invitee.email,
    "invitee_role": invitee.role,
    "invitee_role_id": invitee.roleId,
    "invitee_user_id": invitee.userId,
    "invitee_team_id": invitee.teamId,
    "invitee_team": invitee.teamName,
    "feature_name": featureName,
    "sub_feature_name": subFeatureName,
    "source_url": window.location.href
  },
  "context": { "group_id": org.id },
  "timestamp": new Date().toISOString()
}])
```

---

### feature_used
When: User performs any core product action.

- `event_name` is ALWAYS the literal string `"feature_used"` — never a custom verb
- `feature_name` is always `[noun]_[verb]` snake_case
- `feature_type` must be confirmed with developer before instrumenting — never assume

Ask the developer which applies:
- `"core"` — primary value driver, reason users signed up (e.g. Report builder, Pipeline view)
- `"sticky"` — habit-forming, pulls users back daily/weekly (e.g. Notifications, Workflow automation)
- `"support"` — switching cost builder, embeds product in operations (e.g. SSO, Billing, Audit logs)

```javascript
thriveStack.track([{
  "event_name": "feature_used",
  "user_id": user.id,
  "properties": {
    "user_email": user.email,
    "user_name": user.name,
    "feature_name": "export_report",
    "feature_type": "core",
    "user_role": user.role    // omit if unavailable
  },
  "context": { "group_id": org.id },
  "timestamp": new Date().toISOString()
}])
```

---

## FIRING ORDER

### Direct signup — new account
```
identify → group(Account) → [group(Org/Workspace if present)] → signed_up → account_created → account_added_user
```

### Direct signup — no new account (B2C or account pre-exists)
```
identify → signed_up → account_added_user
```

### Login
```
identify → signed_in
```

### Invite — new user, existing account
```
identify → signed_up → account_added_user
```

### Invite — new user, new account
```
identify → group(Account) → signed_up → account_created → account_added_user
```

### Invite — existing user, not yet in account
```
identify → signed_in → account_added_user
```

### Invite — existing user, already in account
```
No events.
```

### SSO / SCIM / admin provisioning
```
identify → account_added_user
```

---

## VERIFICATION CHECKLIST

Before delivering the summary, verify all 9 points. Fix anything missing first.

- [ ] 1. identify — fires on signup, login, profile/info updates
- [ ] 2. group — fires on entity creation only, correct group_type per level
- [ ] 3. signed_up — correct flow, UTM captured
- [ ] 4. signed_in — fires on every login, context block present
- [ ] 5. account_created — only when new account actually created
- [ ] 6. account_added_user — all applicable entry points covered
- [ ] 7. feature_used — at least one, feature_type confirmed with developer
- [ ] 8. invite_sent — all invite handlers found
- [ ] 9. onboarding_step_completed — all stepper/wizard flows found

### Summary format

Output this summary directly in the chat as your final message. Do not create any files for this summary. Do not write it to markdown. Print it inline.

**ThriveStack Instrumentation Summary**
X/9 implemented

| # | Event | Status | Notes |
|---|-------|--------|-------|
| 1 | identify | ✅/⚠️/❌ | Field used, file |
| 2 | group | ✅/⚠️/❌ | Entity levels, billing root |
| 3 | signed_up | ✅/⚠️/❌ | Handler, file |
| 4 | signed_in | ✅/⚠️/❌ | Handler, file |
| 5 | account_created | ✅/⚠️/❌ | Handler, file or N/A |
| 6 | account_added_user | ✅/⚠️/❌ | Entry points covered |
| 7 | feature_used | ✅/⚠️/❌ | Features and their feature_type |
| 8 | invite_sent | ✅/⚠️/❌ | Handler, file or N/A |
| 9 | onboarding_step_completed | ✅/⚠️/❌ | Steps found or N/A |

**User ID:** [field] in [file]
**Group hierarchy:** [e.g. Organization → Account (billing root) → Workspace] or [B2C fallback]
**Unresolved:** [anything skipped and why, or "None"]

---

**Handy ThriveStack Resources**
- Setup Progress: https://app.thrivestack.ai/analyze/onboarding/product-analytics/telemetry
- Diagnostics: https://app.thrivestack.ai/analyze/diagnostics-report
- API / Audit Logs: https://app.thrivestack.ai/analyze/events
- Developer Tools: https://app.thrivestack.ai/analyze/developer-tool

---

## HARD RULES

1. Global object: `thriveStack` — never `ThriveStack` or `thrivestack`
2. Every method takes an array: `identify([{...}])` `group([{...}])` `track([{...}])`
3. Guard every call: `if (typeof thriveStack === 'undefined') return`
4. timestamp: `new Date().toISOString()` on every call
5. `group_type: "Account"` reserved for billing root only
6. `context.group_id` must equal `group_id` on group() — same variable always
7. `context` required on every `track()` — except `signed_up` when groupId not yet available
8. userId and groupId from auth context only — never hardcode, never generate
9. `api-key` from environment variable only — never in source code
10. Fire-and-forget — never `await` thriveStack calls
11. Fire after action succeeds — inside `.then()` or after `await`
12. `group()` on entity creation only — never on login or context switch
13. `feature_used` is always the literal event_name — never a custom verb
14. `feature_type` confirmed with developer — never assumed
15. `account_added_user` fires in all entry points — signup, invite, SSO/SCIM/admin
16. Do not fire `account_added_user` if user is already a member
17. `user_added_to_account` does not exist — use `account_added_user`
18. Additional properties beyond documented ones are always permitted

---

## CRITICAL MISTAKES

WRONG — missing array:
```javascript
thriveStack.track({ "event_name": "signed_up" })
```
RIGHT:
```javascript
thriveStack.track([{ "event_name": "signed_up" }])
```

WRONG — no guard:
```javascript
thriveStack.identify([{ "user_id": user.id }])
```
RIGHT:
```javascript
if (typeof thriveStack === 'undefined') return
thriveStack.identify([{ "user_id": user.id }])
```

WRONG — group() on login:
```javascript
async function handleLogin() {
  const { user, org } = await auth.signIn(...)
  thriveStack.group([{ "group_id": org.id }])
}
```
RIGHT:
```javascript
async function handleLogin() {
  const { user, org } = await auth.signIn(...)
  thriveStack.identify([{ "user_id": user.id, ... }])
  thriveStack.track([{ "event_name": "signed_in", ... }])
}
```

WRONG — custom event_name for feature:
```javascript
thriveStack.track([{ "event_name": "report_exported" }])
```
RIGHT:
```javascript
thriveStack.track([{
  "event_name": "feature_used",
  "properties": { "feature_name": "report_exported", "feature_type": "core" }
}])
```

WRONG — generating groupId:
```javascript
thriveStack.group([{ "group_id": uuidv4() }])
```
RIGHT:
```javascript
thriveStack.group([{ "group_id": user.orgId }])
```

WRONG — context.group_id mismatch:
```javascript
thriveStack.group([{
  "group_id": org.id,
  "context": { "group_id": uuidv4() }
}])
```
RIGHT:
```javascript
thriveStack.group([{
  "group_id": org.id,
  "context": { "group_id": org.id }
}])
```
